name: compare-table
description: Multi-row comparison table with consistent columns.
purpose: Use when you have 3+ options or 4+ rows of criteria. Wider data than compare-prose can hold legibly.

table.description: Markdown table with header row and 2+ data rows.

skeleton:
<!-- _class: compare-table -->

## Heading framing the comparison.

| Criterion | Option A | Option B | Option C |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First criterion | Value | Value | Value |
| Second criterion | Value | Value | Value |
| Third criterion | Value | Value | Value |
sample:
<!-- _class: compare-table -->

## How the three planning rituals compare.

| Ritual | Cadence | Output | Read after the meeting |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Strategy offsite | Yearly | An 80-slide deck | The title slide |
| Quarterly business review | Quarterly | A scorecard | The red cells |
| Weekly signal review | Weekly | A decision-log entry | Whoever wrote it |

whenToUse.title: Wider than compare-prose
whenToUse.body: Three or more options, or four or more rows of criteria. compare-prose maxes out at two columns and short bodies; compare-table scales further.

whenToUse.title: Cells are short phrases
whenToUse.body: Each cell is a value, a phrase, or a state marker — not a paragraph. If the cells need sentences, use `verdict-grid` or `cards-stack`.

whenToUse.title: Stable column meaning
whenToUse.body: Every row reads the same way across columns. Mixing column meanings row-to-row breaks the table's scannability.

antiPatterns.title: Cells full of prose
antiPatterns.body: Long sentences in a table cell wrap awkwardly and force the column wider. Move to `verdict-grid` for criteria with body text, or `cards-stack` for full prose rows.

antiPatterns.title: More than 6 rows
antiPatterns.body: Past 6 rows the table density crowds the slide. Split into two slides or summarise the rows that don't differentiate.

antiPatterns.title: State-marker rows
antiPatterns.body: When most cells are pass/fail/partial badges, the right layout is `obligation-matrix` or `verdict-grid`. compare-table is for textual values.

related.name: compare-prose
related.when: exactly two options with prose bodies

related.name: verdict-grid
related.when: options scored against criteria with pass/partial/fail badges

related.name: obligation-matrix
related.when: many regimes compared against shared obligations

related.name: cards-stack
related.when: each row needs full-prose breathing room rather than a tabular cell
